Safari & Web.description: Learn how Declarative Web Push can help you deliver notifications more reliably. Find out how to build on existing standards to be more efficient and transparent by design while retaining backwards compatibility with original Web Push.
Safari & Web.transcript_file: Learn more about Declarative Web Push (WWDC25-235).md

Safari & Web.title: Unlock GPU computing with WebGPU
Safari & Web.description: Learn how the WebGPU API provides safe access to GPU devices for graphics and general-purpose computation. We’ll also explore the WGSL shading language to write GPU programs. And we’ll dive into best practices to achieve optimal performance while using minimal power across desktop and mobile devices.

Safari & Web.title: Verify identity documents on the web
Safari & Web.description: Learn how Digital Credentials can enhance online identity verification flows. We’ll cover how websites can integrate the Digital Credentials API to enable requesting information from IDs in Wallet. We’ll also explore how apps can provide their own identity documents for online verification using the new IdentityDocumentServices framework.
Safari & Web.transcript_file: Verify identity documents on the web (WWDC25-232).md

Safari & Web.title: What’s new for the spatial web
Safari & Web.description: Discover the latest spatial features for the web on visionOS 26. We’ll cover how to display inline 3D models with the brand new HTML model element. And we’ll share powerful features, including model lighting, interactions, and animations. Learn how to embed newly supported immersive media on your web site, such as 360-degree video and Apple Immersive Video. And get a sneak peek at adding a custom environment to your web pages.
Safari & Web.transcript_file: What’s new for the spatial web (WWDC25-237).md

Safari & Web.title: What’s new in Safari and WebKit
Safari & Web.description: Learn how the latest web technologies in Safari and WebKit can help you create incredible experiences. We’ll highlight different CSS features and how they work, including scroll driven animation, cross document view transitions, and anchor positioning. We’ll also explore new media support across audio, video, images, and icons.
Safari & Web.transcript_file: What’s new in Safari and WebKit (WWDC25-233).md

Developer Tools.title: Meet Containerization
Developer Tools.description: Meet Containerization, an open source project written in Swift to create and run Linux containers on your Mac. Learn how Containerization approaches Linux containers securely and privately. Discover how the open-sourced Container CLI tool utilizes the Containerization package to provide simple, yet powerful functionality to build, run, and deploy Linux Containers on Mac.

Developer Tools.title: Optimize CPU performance with Instruments
Developer Tools.description: Learn how to optimize your app for Apple silicon with two new hardware-assisted tools in Instruments. We’ll start by covering how to profile your app, then dive deeper by showing every single function called with Processor Trace. We’ll also discuss how to use CPU Counters’ modes to analyze your code for CPU bottlenecks.

Developer Tools.title: Optimize SwiftUI performance with Instruments
Developer Tools.description:
Discover the new SwiftUI instrument. We’ll cover how SwiftUI updates views, how changes in your app’s data affect those updates, and how the new instrument helps you visualize those causes and effects. 

To get the most out of this session, we recommend being familiar with writing apps in SwiftUI.

Developer Tools.title: Profile and optimize power usage in your app
Developer Tools.description: Learn how to optimize your app for maximum battery life. Discover how to identify the root cause of power issues in your app — whether you can reproduce the issue while connected to Xcode or on the go. Find out how to measure power use so you can make better decisions about new features and proactively build more efficient apps.
Developer Tools.transcript_file: Profile and optimize power usage in your app (WWDC25-226).md

Developer Tools.title: Record, replay, and review: UI automation with Xcode
Developer Tools.description: Learn to record, run, and maintain XCUIAutomation tests in Xcode. Replay your XCTest UI tests in dozens of locales, device types, and system conditions using test plan configurations. Review your test results using the Xcode test report, and download screenshots and videos of your runs. We’ll also cover best practices for preparing your app for automation with Accessibility and writing stable, high-quality automation code.
Developer Tools.transcript_file: Record, replay, and review- UI automation with Xcode (WWDC25-344).md

Developer Tools.title: What’s new in Xcode
Developer Tools.description: Discover the latest productivity and performance advancements in Xcode 26. Learn how to leverage large language models in your development workflow. Explore editing and debugging enhancements, improved performance and testing tools, and Swift Build - the open-source build system engine used by Xcode.

Health & Fitness.title: Meet the HealthKit Medications API
Health & Fitness.description: Discover the new Medications API in HealthKit. Explore an example app that uses the new API, learn how to access medications and doses, and find out how your app will manage authorization for this new kind of data.

Health & Fitness.title: Track workouts with HealthKit on iOS and iPadOS
Health & Fitness.description: Learn best practices for building a great workout experience for iOS. Review the life cycle of a workout session, explore the differences between workouts on Apple Watch and iPhone, and find out how to use Live Activities and Siri to pump up your app’s Lock Screen experience.
Health & Fitness.transcript_file: Track workouts with HealthKit on iOS and iPadOS (WWDC25-322).md
